no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-97335425 is an epic (Story 7: artifact listing scale to thousands).

Acceptance criteria: "All child beads closed. Locked decisions from the planning round honored."

Status of children (7 total, all open):
  ddx-b9993722 [open] perf: FEAT-008 + TP-002 measurement contract
  ddx-5681cc57 [open] perf: 2k fixture + baseline non-gating measurement (depends on b9993722)
  ddx-4a7eed8c [open] perf: cache graph/artifact metadata + add search to documents resolver + invalidation (depends on 5681cc57)
  ddx-18b15451 [open] artifacts: wire server-side search in frontend, debounce + stale-response drop
  ddx-ac7ec684 [open] perf: turn measured numbers into gating perf tests
  ddx-0abf27ea [open] perf: lock final FEAT-008 budget numbers (depends on ac7ec684)
  ddx-e315ad80 [open] Story 5/6 contract note on page-local vs corpus-wide

What is done: nothing — this is a tracking epic with no direct deliverable of its own.

What is blocking: the epic cannot close until each child bead is independently executed and closed. Children form a dependency chain (b9993722 -> 5681cc57 -> 4a7eed8c, ac7ec684 -> 0abf27ea); they should be drained in dep order by the queue.

What a follow-up attempt would need: nothing for this bead specifically. The execute-loop will pick up the leaf children (b9993722, 18b15451, e315ad80, ac7ec684) as they become ready. Once all 7 children are closed, this epic can be closed mechanically (no implementation required).

No commit produced; no code change is appropriate for an epic-level bead.
